ingredients.entityContext: A heritage dam observation deck in the Minami-Alps area that appears during Rin's solo touring route in the Ashiyasuantsu valley in Season 2.

ingredients.name: Entrance Square
ingredients.address: Hamana, Imabetsu, Higashitsugaru District, Aomori 030-1501, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: An outdoor area near the Hottarakashi Onsen complex used as a gathering point during the club's Hottarakashi overnight camp.

ingredients.address: Japan, 〒403-0005 Yamanashi, Fujiyoshida, Kamiyoshida, 字剣丸尾5597-84

ingredients.name: Fumotoppara Campground
ingredients.localName: ふもとっぱら(キャンプサイト)
ingredients.entityContext: Rin camps here alone in the opening of Episode 1, discovering her solitary camping style beneath a perfect view of Mt. Fuji that sets the tone for the entire series.

ingredients.entityContext: The day-use hot spring in Komagane where the club stops to warm up during their road trip to the Komagane area in Season 2.

ingredients.name: Hoshakuzan Kozenji Temple
ingredients.entityContext: The historic temple in Komagane lined with cedar trees, shown as a sightseeing stop during the club's Komagane camp trip in Season 2.

ingredients.name: Hottarakashi Onsen
ingredients.entityContext: The open-air hot spring where the Outdoor Activities Club soaks after camping, sharing laughs and nabe leftovers while watching the sun rise over Mt. Fuji.

ingredients.name: Ice cream factory and direct sales store

ingredients.entityContext: The Asagiri Kogen road-station ice cream shop near Fumotoppara, a popular stop for campers heading to the campground that appears in background travel scenes.

ingredients.name: Jinbagatayama Campground
ingredients.address: 1636 Okusa, Nakagawa, Kamiina District, Nagano 399-3801, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Rin visits this remote high-altitude campground with sweeping Ina Valley views during her solo tour in Season 2, quietly admiring the scenery by herself.

ingredients.name: Kanayama Terrace
ingredients.address: 10階, 4 Chome-6-25 Kanayama, Naka Ward, Nagoya, Aichi 460-0022, Japan

ingredients.address: 2926 Nakanokura, Minobu, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-3104, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The campground where Nadeshiko first encounters Rin sleeping by Lake Motosu in Episode 1, sparking the friendship at the heart of the show.

ingredients.name: Koan Main Building Central Lodge

ingredients.name: Nakanokura Tunnel
ingredients.entityContext: The tunnel exit near Lake Motosu that frames the classic Mt. Fuji reflection shot, a scene pilgrimage fans immediately recognize from promotional art and key visuals.

ingredients.name: Mount Fuji YMCA Global Eco Village
ingredients.localName: 富士山YMCA グローバル・エコ・ヴィレッジ
ingredients.entityContext: A lodging facility near Fumotoppara on the Asagiri Plateau referenced as an area landmark during the Fumotoppara camping episodes.

ingredients.name: Motosuko Observation Park
ingredients.address: 2926 Nakanokura, Minobu, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-3104, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The overlook above Lake Motosu where Nadeshiko wakes up to her first breathtaking view of Mt. Fuji, a quietly emotional scene that opens the series.

ingredients.entityContext: The bench in Minobu town where Nadeshiko sits and enthusiastically eats her Minobu manju, a short comic scene pilgrims enjoy re-creating.

ingredients.entityContext: The hilltop viewpoint near Hottarakashi renowned for its night view of the Kofu basin, visited by the club during their overnight Hottarakashi trip.

ingredients.name: Nanbu Bridge
ingredients.description: Nanbu Bridge (南部橋) — local bridge in Minamikoma, Yamanashi, near Utsubuna Park and 南部氏館跡. Not listed on Google Maps, but the coordinates fall in this area — a verified scene location. Navigate by coordinates / Street View.

ingredients.name: Yashajin Hutte
ingredients.entityContext: The mountain hut at Yashajin Pass in the Minami-Alps used as a rest point during Rin's Southern Alps solo ride shown in Season 2.

ingredients.name: Yamanashishi Station
ingredients.entityContext: The station near Hottarakashi Onsen used as a transit point when the club travels to the famous open-air bath and night-view observation area.

ingredients.name: Yamada Farm Fruit Farm

ingredients.entityContext: The fruit orchard near Hottarakashi visited or referenced during the club's Yamanashi City area excursion tied to the Hottarakashi Onsen camp trip.

ingredients.name: Utsubuna Station
ingredients.address: Utsubuna, Nambu, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-2305, Japan

ingredients.name: Takashima Castle

ingredients.name: Takabocchi Mountain

ingredients.localName: マルモ機械 八ヶ岳店
